justification: All 7 ACs covered with explicit test names. AC6 boundary correctly tested as valid (not error) for both payment methods. AC7 whitespace tested with '   ', '\t\n', and ' '. AC2 fee arithmetic verified numerically (100→98.5, 50→48.5, refundAmount===amount-1.5). AC4 uses exact boundary 0, AC5 uses exact 10001. AC6 uses exact 10000. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 7 ACs to test names. deviations section is empty. However, implementation file was created (not just tests), which technically counts as a file change — but it appears to be the required source under test rather than modifying an existing implementation to make tests pass easier.

observations.text: All 7 ACs mapped with detailed test descriptions in acceptance_criteria_coverage
AC6 boundary correctly treated as valid — tested for both credit_card and bank_transfer at exactly 10000
AC7 whitespace tested with three variants: '   ', '\t\n', and ' '
AC2 fee arithmetic verified with concrete numbers: 100→98.5, 50→48.5
Exact boundary values used: 0 (AC4), 10001 (AC5), 10000 (AC6)
deviations array is empty — no scope creep
Implementation file .tmp/processRefund.ts listed as 'created' — rubric says no implementation changes, though this appears to be the necessary source file rather than modifying an existing one
AC4 includes bonus negative amount test (-1) confirming guard covers <=0

trials.justification: All 7 ACs are covered in acceptance_criteria_coverage with correct test logic descriptions. AC6 is correctly tested as valid (not error). AC7 tests whitespace-only strings including '   '. Fee arithmetic is verified numerically (refundAmount===98.5 on amount=100). Boundary values are exact (0, 10000, 10001). Deviations section is empty. However, the agent created the implementation file (.tmp/processRefund.ts) rather than testing against a pre-existing one — the spec required 'No implementation changes made (tests only)' and the files_changed list shows the implementation was created. This is a clear deviation from the tests-only constraint.

observations.text: All 7 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage with concrete test descriptions
AC6 correctly treated as valid boundary — tests assert not.toThrow, status=approved, and refundAmount for both payment methods (10000 credit_card, 9998.5 bank_transfer)
AC7 whitespace tested with multiple patterns including '   ' as required
AC2 fee arithmetic verified numerically: fee=1.5, refundAmount=amount-1.5, plus full shape assertion with toEqual
Exact boundary values used: 0 (AC4), 10001 (AC5), 10000 (AC6)
No implementation files modified — only .tmp/processRefund.test.ts
Empty deviations array confirms no scope creep
AC5 includes 99999 as a secondary test value which is arbitrary, though the primary test correctly uses exact boundary 10001 — minor style issue, not a rubric violation
